latest.title: Camels don't store water in their humps
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you camel humps are fat reserves not water storage that s why they shrink when food s scarce watch the hump sway as they walk those two bumps are pure energy for the next desert sprint did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Crows remember faces for years
latest.script_key: that crow just stared you down notice how it tilts its head side to side crows can remember human faces for years and pass that intel to their flock they re not just being nosy that s their survival tool so next time you meet one give a little nod what s the wildest thing it ll remember about you

latest.title: Monkeys use tree tops to spot danger first
latest.script_key: monkeys aren t just cute they re spies watch how they perch high scanning the forest like tiny towers that s because from up there they see predators before you can blink they re not lollygagging they re staying alive ever wonder why they re always at the top now you know what would you watch for first

latest.title: Chickens don't just cluck they perch in trees
latest.script_key: look at this chicken perched in a tree that s not a turkey chickens do this because they re descended from wild jungle fowl that sleep off the ground to avoid predators notice how their claws wrap tight around the branch that s why they can snooze without falling who knew your breakfast had such good balance
